questions.question: ‘Technology Pioneers Community’ is an initiative of which institution?
questions.explanation: The World Economic Forum said 100 new startups, including five from India, have joined its Technology Pioneers Community. The start-ups include entities from healthcare and financial services to the metaverse. World Economic Forum released the 2022 cohort of Technology Pioneers ahead of its annual meeting in Davos. The five Indian entities include Vahan, SmartCoin Financials, Proeon, Pandocorp and Recykal.

questions.question: Panjshir Valley, which was seen in the news, is located in which country?
questions.explanation: Hundreds of civilians have fled fighting in Afghanistan’s Panjshir Valley after an insurgent group launched an attack against Taliban forces. The Panjshir Valley is known for being a site of resistance by Afghans against Soviet forces in the 1980s and as a base for rebels opposing the Taliban rule of late 1990s. The National Resistance Front (NRF) recently announced an offensive against the Taliban.

questions.question: Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully tested solid rocket booster HS-200, component of which mission?
questions.explanation: Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully tested solid rocket booster HS-200, a key component for the Gaganyaan manned mission programme. The test was conducted at Satish Dhawan Space Centre in Sriharikota. HS-200 booster loaded with 203 tonnes of solid propellant was tested for duration of 135 seconds. The launch vehicle is indigenous developed by ISRO in participation with various industries.

questions.question: Which country is the host of Shanghai Cooperation Organization’s (SCO’s) Regional Anti-Terrorist Structure Meeting in 2022?
questions.explanation: India hosts the meeting of the Shanghai Cooperation Organization’s (SCO’s) anti-terror body–Regional Anti-Terrorist Structure (RATS) in New Delhi. The meeting takes place May 16th to 19th and will see the participation of all members of the group, including Pakistan. ‘RATS’, based at Uzbekistan capital- Tashkent, is a permanent body of the grouping and aims to counter terrorism, extremism, and separatism in the region.

questions.question: What is the theme of the ‘International Day Against Homophobia, Transphobia and Biphobia’ 2022?

options.label: A
options.text: Our Bodies. Our Lives. Our Rights

answer.label: A
answer.text: Our Bodies. Our Lives. Our Rights

questions.explanation: The ‘International Day Against Homophobia, Transphobia and Biphobia’ is observed on 17 May every year. The Day celebrates diversity and raises awareness of the human rights of lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender, and intersex (LGBTIQ+) people. This year’s theme has been chosen to be Our Bodies. Our Lives. Our Rights. According to a recent data, an estimated two billion people live in environments where LGBTIQ+ people are treated as criminals.

questions.question: Sweden and Finland recently signed a formal request to join which global bloc?
questions.explanation: Sweden recently signed a formal request to join NATO, the 30-member military alliance. Finland’s Parliament has also approved NATO membership application. The moves end Sweden’s more than 200 years of military nonalignment and Finland’s nonalignment after World War II.

questions.question: Which regional rural bank bagged six national awards from PFRDA, for achievement in enrolment of Atal Pension Yojana (APY)?
questions.explanation: Karnataka Vikas Grameena Bank (KVGB), a regional rural bank sponsored by Canara Bank, has bagged six national awards for outstanding achievement in enrolment of Atal Pension Yojana (APY). The awards were presented by the Pension Fund Regulatory and Development Authority (PFRDA). The bank had enrolled over 2.6 lakh accounts under the APY totally and during 2021-22, the bank had enrolled over 58,000 accounts as against the target of 39,000.

questions.question: Madden-Julian Oscillation (MJO), which was seen in the news, is associated with which field?
questions.explanation: The major fluctuation in tropical weather on weekly to monthly timescales is named as the Madden-Julian Oscillation (MJO). Recently the Bay of Bengal region was hit by a cyclone named Cyclone Asani that caused rain to the eastern coast of India. This cyclone had a twin named Cyclone Karim in the southern hemisphere. Such twin cyclones are said to be caused by the Madden-Julian Oscillation.

questions.question: RSV, the most common cause of infection in children, is associated with which disease?
questions.explanation: As per a new study, Lower respiratory infection caused by Respiratory Syncytial Virus (RSV) was responsible for more than 1,00,000 deaths in children under five in 2019 across the world. RSV is the most common cause of acute lower respiratory infection in young children. The study was done based on age brackets. It highlighted that there were over 45,000 deaths in infants under six months old in 2019. The incidence rate of RSV in India is 53 per 1,000 children per year.

questions.question: Yagachi River, which was seen in the news, flows in which state?
questions.explanation: The Yagachi reservoir located in the Hassan district of Karnataka was full for the first time in May. Due to the heavy rains in the catchment area, the water level reached 3164.06 ft against the maximum of 3164.90 ft. Yagachi River arises in the Western Ghats near Chickmagaluru, flows through Belur, Hassan District, where it becomes a tributary of the Hemavati River.
